## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Maintenance pace — re-read RELEASE.md with fresh eyes after the rest of the heat lands and apply accumulated fixes the operator identified during heat work but did not address inline. Material gauntlet-design impact: vocabulary, structure, and post-success ceremony all need RELEASE.md updates beyond simple cleanup.
  
  Owns post-success cleanup ceremony placement in RELEASE.md; BBAAH wires the precondition and references this section from README.
  
  ## Work
  
  - Re-read RELEASE.md cold
  - Sweep gauntlet vocabulary: introduce gauntlet-suite framing where the entire test sequence is meant; "pristine" stays for tier-vocabulary and the §1 fixture name
  - Update §3 Sequence to reflect the gauntlet suite shape (suite-of-fixtures composed in order, not custom orchestrator-script)
  - Update Cost framing if drift accumulated
  - No-teardown semantics: explicitly document that rbw-tP leaves canonical infrastructure in place after green tally for operator inspection
  - Document the post-success cleanup ceremony (`rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU`) in RELEASE.md for when operators are ready to release resources
  - Apply other corrections, clarifications, and restructurings captured during the heat but deferred from those paces — sourced from BB heat commit messages (search for RELEASE.md references) and BBAAc walkthrough findings
  - Verify references to rbw-tP, fixture names, and the marshal-zero signature shape match what the heat actually delivered
  - Confirm cross-doc links from RELEASE.md to README.md anchors still resolve
  - Confirm the README's pointer paragraph and reference-tree entry still match RELEASE.md content
  
  ## Verification
  
  - Updates committed
  - Operator confirms the doc reads correctly cold from a release-day perspective
  - Gauntlet vocabulary, no-teardown semantics, and post-success cleanup ceremony all present and clear in RELEASE.md
  - README anchor for cleanup ceremony resolves correctly from BBAAH's reference
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - README.md restructuring beyond cross-doc consistency
  - Re-architecting the release procedure itself (architectural changes belong in a separate heat)

content.text: Let me read the paddock from the gazette file before diving in.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References

148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers

151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAI

## Character

Maintenance pace — re-read RELEASE.md with fresh eyes after the rest of the heat lands and apply accumulated fixes the operator identified during heat work but did not address inline. Material gauntlet-design impact: vocabulary, structure, and post-success ceremony all need RELEASE.md updates beyond simple cleanup.

Owns post-success cleanup ceremony placement in RELEASE.md; BBAAH wires the precondition and references this section from README.

- Verify references to rbw-tP, fixture names, and the marshal-zero signature shape match what the heat actually delivered
- Confirm cross-doc links from RELEASE.md to README.md anchors still resolve
- Confirm the README's pointer paragraph and reference-tree entry still match RELEASE.md content

## Verification

## Out of scope

### rbs0-sync-sweep-from-bb-changes (₢BBAAq) [rough]

## Character

Reflective audit pace at the tail of BB. Walk the changes landed across BB's paces and determine which RBS0 (and adjacent veiled-spec) sections fell out of sync with code/behavior, then update them.

Cognitive posture: deliberate read-and-compare. Source of truth is the code/handbook surface as it stands at mount-time; RBS0 is being reconciled to it.

## Wrap criterion

RBS0 (and any directly-implicated sibling spec files under `rbk/vov_veiled/`) reflect the behavior shipped during BB. Each touched section either updated or explicitly judged still-correct. No silent drift left behind for the next heat.

## Constraints

- Read-only against code; spec edits only.
- Scope is BB's changes — not a full RBS0 audit.

## Out of scope

- Restructuring RBS0 beyond what BB's changes require.
- Spec touches outside the veiled-spec family unless directly implicated.

## Known sync points (running list — append as paces land)

- **`RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc`** (from BBAAc inline fix on the JWT-SA probe — IAM-policy-read propagation race absorption). The procedural spec describes the OLD semantic: "all iterations must pass; HTTP 403 → fatal access denied; HTTP 401 → fatal token rejected." New code semantic in `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`: bounded propagation-absorbing retry — first 2xx wins, HTTP 403 retries within `max_attempts × delay_ms` budget, all other codes (including 401) fall to a single fail-fast catch-all with body-summary diagnostic via `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture`. Sections needing rewrite: §"Execute Probe Iterations" (rename concept to *attempts*; rewrite "Evaluate Response" sub-section to reflect 2xx-wins / 403-retry / else-fatal); §"Display Summary" ("succeeded after «I» attempt(s)" instead of "«N»/«N» iterations passed"); the top NOTE block (intent shift from sustained-access verification to propagation-window absorption + verification); §"Validate Input Parameters" (`ITERATION_COUNT` description shifts to "max attempts — first 2xx wins"; `DELAY_MS` to "between retries"). Companion call-site change: `rbte_engine.sh::rbte_probe` defaults shifted to 60 × 3000ms (≈180s budget) for SA roles only; Payor probe defaults and semantic unchanged.

### fast-qualify-shellcheck-fold-cover-zero (₢BBABG) [rough]

## Character

Mechanical. Fold the two fast-codebase-check entry points into one tier, parallelize the slow one, expand its coverage, bring its baseline to zero, and wire it into marshal-zero as an early structural gate.

## Locked decisions

- **One fast-tier shellcheck entry.** `rbq_qualify_fast` absorbs `buq_shellcheck`; standalone `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` retires. The primitive function stays; only the standalone tabtarget retires. `rbw-tr` drops its now-redundant shellcheck phase.
- **Parallelize via xargs -P.** Replace the single shellcheck invocation with xargs-driven parallel invocation; aggregate exit code into one verdict. Target ~1–2s wall-clock for the file set (down from ~9s).
- **Coverage expansion.** `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh` joins the shellcheck file set. Mount-time call on scope-generalization shape (multi-root parameter vs explicit outside-Tools list).
- **Baseline zero.** Every current shellcheck finding either fixed in-place or moved to `busc_shellcheckrc` with BCG-structural rationale. Fast-tier exits zero on clean tree after wrap.
- **MZ early gate.** `rblm_zero` runs the parallelized shellcheck primitive in its pre-check block (alongside tree-clean / upstream / pushed). Non-zero shellcheck fails marshal-zero before it touches anything. Gauntlet entry-state cannot accept a shellcheck-dirty codebase by construction.

## Wrap criterion

`tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` exits zero, runs in ~2s wall-clock, covers `Tools/` plus `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh`. `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` no longer exists. `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` no longer makes a separate shellcheck call. `tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` fails fast on any shellcheck finding before mutating anything.

## Out of scope

- `tt/*.sh` shellcheck coverage (`buq_tabtargets` shape-check is the appropriate gate for generated shims)
- `.buk/launcher.*.sh` (3–4 line trivial shims; capture as itch if ever produces a finding)
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/` subtree — operator call at mount (exempt vs fix)

### foundry-account-state-invalid-tolerance (₢BBABL) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical retry-tolerance extension; shape locked by the memo. Two touchpoints, one error class. Surfaced by dogfight verification (2 failures / 3 runs — transient).

## Source
`Memos/memo-20260527-account-state-invalid-reinvest-flap.md` — full 401/`ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` signature, two-host evidence, the prior-art gap, and the time-bound tolerance model to reuse.

## Shape (locked)
- `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` build-status poll loop: absorb a transient `401 UNAUTHENTICATED` + `ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` with bounded backoff instead of counting it toward the consecutive-error fatal — a still-`WORKING` build must survive a director-SA auth flap.
- `rbgo_OAuth.sh` consumer-retry token mint: extend the existing retry error-class to `ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID`, covering the `rbfv` vouch path (Host B in the memo).
- The retry budget is the discriminator (settling completes within budget; real denial waits it out and fails clean) — reuse the established propagation / access-probe time-bound model rather than minting new literals.

## Done
A director re-invest immediately followed by `ordain` survives the flap: the transient is logged and retried within budget, never fatal on a healthy build. Confirm the `rbfv` vouch failure body is the same class (memo caveat).

### rbw-tP-first-run (₢BBAAX) [rough]

## Character

First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; this pace exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

Post-BBABB landing: this pace may run concurrently with BBABA on a different machine when both stations carry distinct BURS_TINCTURE values. Tincture composition makes RBDC-derived names disjoint per station; the remaining collision surface is operator error (same tincture on both stations).

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Scope

- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; required for the parallel-station property)

## Out of scope

- Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios
- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

### rbw-tP-branched-rerun (₢BBABA) [rough]

## Character

Validate BBAAX's repairs by re-running rbw-tP end-to-end on a branch named after this pace's coronet. Branch isolation lets parallel attempts on different machines avoid the commit-trail race on main; with BBABB landed, parallel mac+linux execution against a shared payor manor is now first-class — distinct BURS_TINCTURE values give every RBDC-derived name (depot project_id, GAR repo, GCS bucket, SA emails) per-station disjointness by construction.

## Wrap criterion

Green tally end-to-end. Same guardrail as BBAAX — partial completion is not pass.

## Cost

~90m wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt.

## Scope

- Create a branch named after this pace's coronet, off current main
- Run rbw-tP from marshal-zero on the branch
- Operator selects which machine the pace mounts on (mac or linux); pace does NOT use foray
- Triage surprises per BBAAX rules: tiny inline fix + re-run, larger → follow-up pace

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Locked decisions

- Branch name == pace coronet (identity-stable; no human-meaningful name)
- Branch lives until pace wraps; deletion is operator territory

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; the parallel-station property depends on it)

toolUseResult.text:
Disk survey (threshold 85%): 228 GB: 61%
Heat: rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification (₣BB)
Status: racing
Created: 260426


## Paces

### release-doc-revision (₢BBAAI) [rough]

## Character

Maintenance pace — re-read RELEASE.md with fresh eyes after the rest of the heat lands and apply accumulated fixes the operator identified during heat work but did not address inline. Material gauntlet-design impact: vocabulary, structure, and post-success ceremony all need RELEASE.md updates beyond simple cleanup.

Owns post-success cleanup ceremony placement in RELEASE.md; BBAAH wires the precondition and references this section from README.

## Work

- Re-read RELEASE.md cold
- Sweep gauntlet vocabulary: introduce gauntlet-suite framing where the entire test sequence is meant; "pristine" stays for tier-vocabulary and the §1 fixture name
- Update §3 Sequence to reflect the gauntlet suite shape (suite-of-fixtures composed in order, not custom orchestrator-script)
- Update Cost framing if drift accumulated
- No-teardown semantics: explicitly document that rbw-tP leaves canonical infrastructure in place after green tally for operator inspection
- Document the post-success cleanup ceremony (`rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU`) in RELEASE.md for when operators are ready to release resources
- Apply other corrections, clarifications, and restructurings captured during the heat but deferred from those paces — sourced from BB heat commit messages (search for RELEASE.md references) and BBAAc walkthrough findings
- Verify references to rbw-tP, fixture names, and the marshal-zero signature shape match what the heat actually delivered
- Confirm cross-doc links from RELEASE.md to README.md anchors still resolve
- Confirm the README's pointer paragraph and reference-tree entry still match RELEASE.md content

## Verification

- Updates committed
- Operator confirms the doc reads correctly cold from a release-day perspective
- Gauntlet vocabulary, no-teardown semantics, and post-success cleanup ceremony all present and clear in RELEASE.md
- README anchor for cleanup ceremony resolves correctly from BBAAH's reference

## Out of scope

- README.md restructuring beyond cross-doc consistency
- Re-architecting the release procedure itself (architectural changes belong in a separate heat)

### rbs0-sync-sweep-from-bb-changes (₢BBAAq) [rough]

## Character

Reflective audit pace at the tail of BB. Walk the changes landed across BB's paces and determine which RBS0 (and adjacent veiled-spec) sections fell out of sync with code/behavior, then update them.

Cognitive posture: deliberate read-and-compare. Source of truth is the code/handbook surface as it stands at mount-time; RBS0 is being reconciled to it.

## Wrap criterion

RBS0 (and any directly-implicated sibling spec files under `rbk/vov_veiled/`) reflect the behavior shipped during BB. Each touched section either updated or explicitly judged still-correct. No silent drift left behind for the next heat.

## Constraints

- Read-only against code; spec edits only.
- Scope is BB's changes — not a full RBS0 audit.

## Out of scope

- Restructuring RBS0 beyond what BB's changes require.
- Spec touches outside the veiled-spec family unless directly implicated.

## Known sync points (running list — append as paces land)

- **`RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc`** (from BBAAc inline fix on the JWT-SA probe — IAM-policy-read propagation race absorption). The procedural spec describes the OLD semantic: "all iterations must pass; HTTP 403 → fatal access denied; HTTP 401 → fatal token rejected." New code semantic in `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`: bounded propagation-absorbing retry — first 2xx wins, HTTP 403 retries within `max_attempts × delay_ms` budget, all other codes (including 401) fall to a single fail-fast catch-all with body-summary diagnostic via `zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture`. Sections needing rewrite: §"Execute Probe Iterations" (rename concept to *attempts*; rewrite "Evaluate Response" sub-section to reflect 2xx-wins / 403-retry / else-fatal); §"Display Summary" ("succeeded after «I» attempt(s)" instead of "«N»/«N» iterations passed"); the top NOTE block (intent shift from sustained-access verification to propagation-window absorption + verification); §"Validate Input Parameters" (`ITERATION_COUNT` description shifts to "max attempts — first 2xx wins"; `DELAY_MS` to "between retries"). Companion call-site change: `rbte_engine.sh::rbte_probe` defaults shifted to 60 × 3000ms (≈180s budget) for SA roles only; Payor probe defaults and semantic unchanged.

### fast-qualify-shellcheck-fold-cover-zero (₢BBABG) [rough]

## Character

Mechanical. Fold the two fast-codebase-check entry points into one tier, parallelize the slow one, expand its coverage, bring its baseline to zero, and wire it into marshal-zero as an early structural gate.

## Locked decisions

- **One fast-tier shellcheck entry.** `rbq_qualify_fast` absorbs `buq_shellcheck`; standalone `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` retires. The primitive function stays; only the standalone tabtarget retires. `rbw-tr` drops its now-redundant shellcheck phase.
- **Parallelize via xargs -P.** Replace the single shellcheck invocation with xargs-driven parallel invocation; aggregate exit code into one verdict. Target ~1–2s wall-clock for the file set (down from ~9s).
- **Coverage expansion.** `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh` joins the shellcheck file set. Mount-time call on scope-generalization shape (multi-root parameter vs explicit outside-Tools list).
- **Baseline zero.** Every current shellcheck finding either fixed in-place or moved to `busc_shellcheckrc` with BCG-structural rationale. Fast-tier exits zero on clean tree after wrap.
- **MZ early gate.** `rblm_zero` runs the parallelized shellcheck primitive in its pre-check block (alongside tree-clean / upstream / pushed). Non-zero shellcheck fails marshal-zero before it touches anything. Gauntlet entry-state cannot accept a shellcheck-dirty codebase by construction.

## Wrap criterion

`tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` exits zero, runs in ~2s wall-clock, covers `Tools/` plus `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh`. `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` no longer exists. `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` no longer makes a separate shellcheck call. `tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh` fails fast on any shellcheck finding before mutating anything.

## Out of scope

- `tt/*.sh` shellcheck coverage (`buq_tabtargets` shape-check is the appropriate gate for generated shims)
- `.buk/launcher.*.sh` (3–4 line trivial shims; capture as itch if ever produces a finding)
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/` subtree — operator call at mount (exempt vs fix)

### foundry-account-state-invalid-tolerance (₢BBABL) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical retry-tolerance extension; shape locked by the memo. Two touchpoints, one error class. Surfaced by dogfight verification (2 failures / 3 runs — transient).

## Source
`Memos/memo-20260527-account-state-invalid-reinvest-flap.md` — full 401/`ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` signature, two-host evidence, the prior-art gap, and the time-bound tolerance model to reuse.

## Shape (locked)
- `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` build-status poll loop: absorb a transient `401 UNAUTHENTICATED` + `ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` with bounded backoff instead of counting it toward the consecutive-error fatal — a still-`WORKING` build must survive a director-SA auth flap.
- `rbgo_OAuth.sh` consumer-retry token mint: extend the existing retry error-class to `ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID`, covering the `rbfv` vouch path (Host B in the memo).
- The retry budget is the discriminator (settling completes within budget; real denial waits it out and fails clean) — reuse the established propagation / access-probe time-bound model rather than minting new literals.

## Done
A director re-invest immediately followed by `ordain` survives the flap: the transient is logged and retried within budget, never fatal on a healthy build. Confirm the `rbfv` vouch failure body is the same class (memo caveat).

### rbw-tP-first-run (₢BBAAX) [rough]

## Character

First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; this pace exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

Post-BBABB landing: this pace may run concurrently with BBABA on a different machine when both stations carry distinct BURS_TINCTURE values. Tincture composition makes RBDC-derived names disjoint per station; the remaining collision surface is operator error (same tincture on both stations).

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Scope

- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; required for the parallel-station property)

## Out of scope

- Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios
- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

### rbw-tP-branched-rerun (₢BBABA) [rough]

## Character

Validate BBAAX's repairs by re-running rbw-tP end-to-end on a branch named after this pace's coronet. Branch isolation lets parallel attempts on different machines avoid the commit-trail race on main; with BBABB landed, parallel mac+linux execution against a shared payor manor is now first-class — distinct BURS_TINCTURE values give every RBDC-derived name (depot project_id, GAR repo, GCS bucket, SA emails) per-station disjointness by construction.

## Wrap criterion

Green tally end-to-end. Same guardrail as BBAAX — partial completion is not pass.

## Cost

~90m wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt.

## Scope

- Create a branch named after this pace's coronet, off current main
- Run rbw-tP from marshal-zero on the branch
- Operator selects which machine the pace mounts on (mac or linux); pace does NOT use foray
- Triage surprises per BBAAX rules: tiny inline fix + re-run, larger → follow-up pace

## Collision-aware diagnostic

Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.

## Locked decisions

- Branch name == pace coronet (identity-stable; no human-meaningful name)
- Branch lives until pace wraps; deletion is operator territory

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; the parallel-station property depends on it)

content.text:
Heat **₣BB** (rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification) — **59/65 done, 6 remaining**, all marked `[rough]`. Here's what each remaining pace is about, in heat order:

---

**1. ₢BBAAI — release-doc-revision**
Fresh-eyes revision of RELEASE.md now that the heat has landed. Sweep in gauntlet-suite vocabulary, rewrite §3 Sequence as suite-of-fixtures (not a custom orchestrator), document the no-teardown semantics and the operator-driven post-success cleanup ceremony (`rbw-fA`/`rbw-arD`/`rbw-adD`/`rbw-dU`). Verify references and RELEASE↔README cross-doc anchors. *(This is the one you just declined to mount.)*

**2. ₢BBAAq — rbs0-sync-sweep-from-bb-changes**
Reflective spec audit at the tail. Walk what BB's paces changed and reconcile RBS0 (and sibling veiled specs under `rbk/vov_veiled/`) to the shipped code/behavior. Read-only against code; spec edits only; scoped to BB's changes, not a full RBS0 audit. Known sync point already logged: **RBSAJ-access_jwt_probe.adoc** — its procedural spec still describes the old "all iterations pass / 403 fatal" semantic, but the code now does bounded propagation-absorbing retry (first 2xx wins, 403 retries within budget, else fail-fast).

**3. ₢BBABG — fast-qualify-shellcheck-fold-cover-zero**
Mechanical. Fold the two fast-codebase-check entry points into one tier: `rbq_qualify_fast` absorbs `buq_shellcheck`, retire standalone `tt/buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh`, `rbw-tr` drops its redundant shellcheck phase. Parallelize via `xargs -P` (target ~2s wall-clock from ~9s), expand coverage to `.buk/rbbc_constants.sh`, bring baseline to zero, and wire the parallel shellcheck into `rblm_zero` as an early marshal-zero gate so a shellcheck-dirty tree can't enter the gauntlet by construction.

**4. ₢BBABL — foundry-account-state-invalid-tolerance**
Mechanical retry-tolerance extension, shape locked by `memo-20260527-account-state-invalid-reinvest-flap.md`. Surfaced by dogfight (2 failures / 3 runs, transient). Two touchpoints: `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` build-status poll loop absorbs a transient `401 ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` with bounded backoff (a still-`WORKING` build survives a director-SA auth flap); `rbgo_OAuth.sh` consumer-retry extends its error class to cover the `rbfv` vouch path. Retry budget is the discriminator.

**5. ₢BBAAX — rbw-tP-first-run**
The payoff pace. First end-to-end run of the gauntlet suite via `rbw-tP` against fresh state — where the cross-section silent-first-build bugs surface that case-level probes couldn't. **Wrap criterion is a green tally**, explicitly guarded against "I built it" drift. ~1 hour + 2 GCP projects per attempt. Capture every surprise; tiny fixes inline + re-run, larger ones spawn follow-up paces. Mid-qualification failure = start over from marshal-zero.

**6. ₢BBABA — rbw-tP-branched-rerun**
Validate BBAAX's repairs by re-running `rbw-tP` end-to-end on a branch named after this pace's coronet. With BURS_TINCTURE composition landed, parallel mac+linux execution against a shared payor manor is first-class (tincture gives every RBDC-derived name per-station disjointness). No foray — operator picks the machine. Same green-tally guardrail. ~90m + 2 GCP projects per attempt.

---

A shape note worth surfacing: the four `AA*`/`AB*` paces (doc/spec/shellcheck/retry-tolerance) are all preparation and cleanup, while the real gate is the last two — **₢BBAAX then ₢BBABA**, the actual pristine runs. Want to groom further (reorder, reslate any docket, adjust scope), or mount one of these?

content: Heat ₣BB (release qualification) is 59/65 done with 6 paces left, all prep and cleanup except the two final pristine runs. Next action: mount one of the remaining paces, likely ₢BBAAX (the first end-to-end rbw-tP run). (disable recaps in /config)

In fact, groom AU: maybe we need to create a 'fresh' heat to remember the key ideas for final release preparation, probably paddock only until we get close to running that heat.

content.text: Let me read the AU paddock from the gazette.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣AU
2	
3	## Paddock: rbk-14-mvp-release-finalize
4	
5	## Context
6	
7	A small cleanup-and-validation heat at the tail of the Recipe Bottle release
8	effort. Its original charter — supply-chain trust hardening plus
9	release-qualification tooling — has largely shipped or migrated. The
10	release-qualification charter now lives in the rbk-16 heat (₣BB): the
11	marshal-zero → gauntlet/pristine model, the RELEASE.md runbook, and the
12	prep-release ceremony all belong there. The prep-release ceremony rewrite and
13	the foundry/cloud-step trust audit that once lived here were restrung into ₣BB
14	so the release ceremony and the foundry surface each have a single owner.
15	
16	What remains in ₣AU is genuinely local: developer-facing hygiene and end-to-end
17	validation that do not depend on the gauntlet model.
18	
19	## Remaining Scope
20	
21	- **Regime-render error hygiene** — every regime render/validate/info function
22	  should fail with an actionable parameter message, not a terse "module not
23	  kindled" crash or a set -u unbound-variable death. Includes a sweep test
24	  fixture that exercises every render/info and asserts clean exit, plus logging
25	  discipline for batch-sweep context.
26	- **Onboarding end-to-end walk** — exercise every handbook track in sequence
27	  from a learner's perspective; confirm windows render with prefixed resource
28	  names and probe outputs report the correct prefixed identifiers.
29	- **openssl base64 helper factoring** — the six base64 sites that ₣BB's base64
30	  sweep unified on openssl still carry a duplicated invocation; factor into
31	  rbgu_Utility.sh helpers, or close won't-fix with rationale recorded.
32	- **Small doc/file hygiene** — relocate the compose-probe sentinel out of the
33	  .rbk/ runtime config directory into Tools/rbk/ (the file move itself already
34	  appears done — verify references before closing); and a BCG clarification
35	  distinguishing the `$(<file)` builtin redirect from command substitution
36	  (verify against the landed substitution-rule refinement first — may already
37	  be covered).
38	
39	## Relationship to other heats
40	
41	- **₣BB (rbk-16-mvp-release-qualification)** — the live release-qualification
42	  heat; inherited this heat's prep-release ceremony work and the foundry trust
43	  audit. The audit is sequenced at ₣BB's tail so it reviews the post-reshaping
44	  foundry surface rather than transitional code.
45	- **₣Ak (rbk-mvp-1, retired)** — prior art for the directory/spec/test
46	  structure.
47	
48	## Deferred technical debt (post-release itch candidates)
49	
50	Catalogued during this heat's BCG-compliance work; deferred, not lost. Better
51	homed in jji_itch.md than in the paddock long-term:
52	
53	- `z_` prefix on locals — naming convention, no runtime impact (pervasive)
54	- Unbraced `"$var"` expansions — mechanical
55	- `2>/dev/null` stderr suppression — case-by-case judgment
56	- `local -r` missing on single-assignment locals — pervasive
57	- Raw `echo` for user messages in display-heavy modules
58	- Bare file truncation (`> file` without `:`)
59	
60	## References
61	
62	- Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh — qualification orchestrator
63	- Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh — core `buv_render()` engine
64	- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md — command-substitution rules
65	- Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh — utility home for the base64 helpers
66	- Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc — main specification
67

# jjezs_paddock ₣AU

## Paddock: rbk-14-mvp-release-finalize

## Context

A small cleanup-and-validation heat at the tail of the Recipe Bottle release
effort. Its original charter — supply-chain trust hardening plus
release-qualification tooling — has largely shipped or migrated. The
release-qualification charter now lives in the rbk-16 heat (₣BB): the
marshal-zero → gauntlet/pristine model, the RELEASE.md runbook, and the
prep-release ceremony all belong there. The prep-release ceremony rewrite and
the foundry/cloud-step trust audit that once lived here were restrung into ₣BB
so the release ceremony and the foundry surface each have a single owner.

What remains in ₣AU is genuinely local: developer-facing hygiene and end-to-end
validation that do not depend on the gauntlet model.

## Remaining Scope

- **Regime-render error hygiene** — every regime render/validate/info function
  should fail with an actionable parameter message, not a terse "module not
  kindled" crash or a set -u unbound-variable death. Includes a sweep test
  fixture that exercises every render/info and asserts clean exit, plus logging
  discipline for batch-sweep context.
- **Onboarding end-to-end walk** — exercise every handbook track in sequence
  from a learner's perspective; confirm windows render with prefixed resource
  names and probe outputs report the correct prefixed identifiers.
- **openssl base64 helper factoring** — the six base64 sites that ₣BB's base64
  sweep unified on openssl still carry a duplicated invocation; factor into
  rbgu_Utility.sh helpers, or close won't-fix with rationale recorded.
- **Small doc/file hygiene** — relocate the compose-probe sentinel out of the
  .rbk/ runtime config directory into Tools/rbk/ (the file move itself already
  appears done — verify references before closing); and a BCG clarification
  distinguishing the `$(<file)` builtin redirect from command substitution
  (verify against the landed substitution-rule refinement first — may already
  be covered).

## Relationship to other heats

- **₣BB (rbk-16-mvp-release-qualification)** — the live release-qualification
  heat; inherited this heat's prep-release ceremony work and the foundry trust
  audit. The audit is sequenced at ₣BB's tail so it reviews the post-reshaping
  foundry surface rather than transitional code.
- **₣Ak (rbk-mvp-1, retired)** — prior art for the directory/spec/test
  structure.

## Deferred technical debt (post-release itch candidates)

Catalogued during this heat's BCG-compliance work; deferred, not lost. Better
homed in jji_itch.md than in the paddock long-term:

- `z_` prefix on locals — naming convention, no runtime impact (pervasive)
- Unbraced `"$var"` expansions — mechanical
- `2>/dev/null` stderr suppression — case-by-case judgment
- `local -r` missing on single-assignment locals — pervasive
- Raw `echo` for user messages in display-heavy modules
- Bare file truncation (`> file` without `:`)

## References

- Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh — qualification orchestrator
- Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh — core `buv_render()` engine
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md — command-substitution rules
- Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh — utility home for the base64 helpers
- Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc — main specification

# jjezs_pace ₢AUAA7

Drafted from ₢A_AAG in ₣A_.

## Character
End-to-end validation — user perspective. Every handbook track must complete cleanly.

## Work
Walk every onboarding track in sequence: Crash Course, Credential Retriever, Credential Director, First Crucible, Director First Cloud Build, Payor, Governor. Verify all handbook windows render correctly with prefixed resource names. Confirm probe outputs show the correct prefixed container/image identifiers. Fix any display or functional issues.

content.text:
Read the AU paddock. Here's the picture, and I want to push back gently on one part of the framing before we start moving things.

## ₣AU as it actually stands

**rbk-18-mvp-release-finalize, 42/43 done.** One enrolled pace remains:

- **₢AUAA7 — run-all-onboarding-tracks** `[rough]`: end-to-end user-perspective walk of every handbook track (Crash Course → Credential Retriever/Director → First Crucible → Director First Cloud Build → Payor → Governor), confirming windows render with prefixed resource names and probes report correct prefixed identifiers.

But the paddock's "Remaining Scope" lists **four** items, only one of which (the onboarding walk) is actually enrolled as a pace:
1. Regime-render error hygiene — not enrolled
2. Onboarding end-to-end walk — = ₢AUAA7
3. openssl base64 helper factoring — not enrolled, **and likely already moot** (the paddock itself notes ₣BB's base64 sweep unified these; this would be residual factoring at most)
4. Small doc/file hygiene (compose-probe relocate, BCG `$(<file)` clarification) — not enrolled, paddock says both "may already be done — verify"

So AU is already drifted: the paddock remembers more than the ledger holds, and most of that memory is either stale or done elsewhere.

## What I see across the whole release surface

You have **three** stale release heats racing simultaneously, and their paddocks cross-reference each other with *stale numbering* (AU's paddock calls itself "rbk-14" and BB "rbk-16", but the silks say rbk-18 and rbk-15). That naming drift is itself a symptom — the release effort got reshaped repeatedly and the records didn't keep up:

- **₣BB** rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification — 59/65 (the gauntlet/pristine machinery)
- **₣AU** rbk-18-mvp-release-finalize — 42/43 (cleanup + onboarding walk)
- **₣A6** rbk-17-mvp-handbook-restart — 46/57 (didn't open it, but "handbook-restart" overlaps AU's onboarding walk)

## Where I'd push back

You called the BB paces "stale," and for **three of the six** that's exactly right — they're stale *by timing*, gated on "are we actually releasing":
- BBAAI release-doc-revision (RELEASE.md polish — premature if the release model is being rethought)
- BBAAX rbw-tP-first-run (the actual pristine gauntlet run)
- BBABA rbw-tP-branched-rerun (the rerun)

But the **other three are not release ceremony at all** — they're real engineering that stands on its own regardless of release timing:
- **BBABL foundry-account-state-invalid-tolerance** — a genuine *bug fix* (401/`ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` flap, 2 failures / 3 dogfight runs, shape locked by a memo). This should not get swept into a "drop most" gesture.
- **BBABG fast-qualify-shellcheck-fold** — mechanical tooling improvement (parallelize shellcheck, marshal-zero gate). Independent value.
- **BBAAq rbs0-sync-sweep** — real spec debt with a concrete known sync point (RBSAJ describes the old probe semantic; code already changed).

If we drop those three along with the timing-stale ones, we lose a bug fix and two pieces of standing debt.

## Proposed shape (not executed — your call)

1. **New stabled heat, paddock-only** — the durable memory you described. Its paddock distills the *surviving* release ideas: the marshal-zero → gauntlet/pristine model, the entry-contract refusal, no-teardown + cleanup ceremony, the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage. The two pristine-run paces (BBAAX, BBABA) and the doc-revision (BBAAI) get described there in prose, not enrolled as live paces. Stays stabled until you're close to release.
2. **Relocate the three real-debt paces** out of the release heats into wherever they belong active (a maintenance heat, or keep them if BB stays open for them) — `jjx_relocate` preserves their dockets rather than re-authoring.
3. **AU**: keep or move ₢AUAA7 (it overlaps A6 — worth deciding together); prune the dead paddock scope items.
4. Then retire ₣BB and ₣AU once emptied.

Before I touch anything: this is structural and irreversible-ish, so I want your direction on two forks.

Clear. Here's the plan I'll execute:

- **₣BB stays racing**, narrowed to its three real-debt paces (BBABL bug fix, BBABG shellcheck fold, BBAAq spec sync) for near-term execution. Drop the three timing-stale paces (BBAAI, BBAAX, BBABA) **after** their ideas are preserved.

input.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BU

## Purpose

Durable memory for **final release preparation** of Recipe Bottle. This heat is
deliberately **paddock-only and stabled** until the project is close to actually
cutting a release. It exists so the hard-won release model — and the specific
runs and doc work that gate a release — are not lost while the release itself
is deferred.

When release time comes: re-read this paddock, slate fresh paces for the runs
and doc revision described below (dockets in git history of the dropped ₣BB
paces if detail is needed), confirm the precursor engineering has landed, and
race the heat.

## What already shipped (do not re-do)

The release-qualification **machinery** is built and lives under ₣BB:

- `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — the release gate tabtarget.
- The **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator) composing
  fixtures in a state ladder from marshal-zero through canonical-credentialed
  to crucible verification.
- The marshal-zero signature gate baked into `rblm_zero` (`rbw-MZ`).

This heat does not rebuild any of that. It is about *running* it and finishing
the operator-facing documentation around it.

## The release-qualification model (the idea worth keeping)

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. It **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was
just committed — the refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or
operator discipline. This is what catches the silent-first-build bug class
(k-prefixed hallmarks, project-name prefix omissions, reliquary-integrity on
negative tests) that every accumulated-state tier silently tolerates.

**Entry contract** (all must hold or `rbw-tP` fails fast): working tree clean;
HEAD is a marshal-zero commit; RBRR fields blank; no RBRA credential files
present (governor/retriever/director — minted by the qualification, not restored
from backup); no hallmark fields in nameplates; no depot-scoped fields in vessel
rbrv.env files. Only **Payor OAuth** is the operator's prerequisite; everything
else is self-minted.

**Release-branch execution contract** — run on a release branch, not main; the
branch accumulates first-class commits during the run (the growing commit trail
is the operator's mental anchor against losing their place in a long sequence);
stop on the very first failure, cleanly. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh
marshal-zero — **mid-qualification failure means start over from zero, not
patch-and-continue**. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches. Any
scaffolding for partial-run cleanup or multi-mode failure handling is
non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code.

**No automatic teardown.** After a green tally the canonical depot, SAs,
hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Cleanup is
operator-driven: `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU`.

## Pending release work (slate as paces when close)

### Pristine first run

First end-to-end run of the gauntlet suite via `rbw-tP` against actual fresh
state — where cross-section bugs that case-level probes can't see finally
surface. **Wrap criterion is a green tally**, not "ran to completion" — an
explicit guardrail against the agent failure mode of wrapping at "I built it"
rather than "it works against reality." Cost ≈ 1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP
projects per attempt. Capture every surprise; triage tiny fixes inline + re-run,
larger ones into follow-up paces. Re-runs draw fresh quota; budget accordingly.

### Pristine branched re-run (validation)

Re-run `rbw-tP` end-to-end on a release branch to validate the first run's
repairs. With BURS_TINCTURE composition landed, parallel mac+linux execution
against a shared Payor manor is first-class — distinct tincture values give every
RBDC-derived name (depot project_id, GAR repo, GCS bucket, SA emails) per-station
disjointness by construction. Operator picks the machine; no foray. Same
green-tally guardrail.

**Collision-aware diagnostic** (both runs): any GCP "resource already exists"
error should name BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term with a hypothesis pointing
at peer-station tincture overlap — distinguishing tincture-composition collision
from a transient GCP race.

### RELEASE.md revision

Re-read RELEASE.md cold and bring it into alignment with what the gauntlet suite
became:

- Gauntlet-suite vocabulary (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case; "pristine" reserved for the
  §1 fixture and tier vocabulary).
- §3 Sequence rewritten as suite-of-fixtures composed in order, not a custom
  orchestrator script.
- No-teardown semantics documented explicitly.
- The post-success cleanup ceremony (`rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` +
  `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU`) documented for when operators are ready to release
  resources.
- Verify rbw-tP / fixture-name / marshal-zero-signature references match what
  shipped; confirm RELEASE.md ↔ README.md cross-doc anchors resolve.
- Source deferred corrections from ₣BB commit messages (grep RELEASE.md refs)
  and the pristine-walkthrough findings.

The `/rbk-prep-release` ceremony treats a pristine pass as a precondition; the
README points into the cleanup-ceremony section. Keep those cross-doc links live.

### Onboarding end-to-end walk (carried from ₣AU)

Walk every handbook track in sequence from a learner's perspective — Crash
Course, Credential Retriever, Credential Director, First Crucible, Director First
Cloud Build, Payor, Governor — confirming windows render with prefixed resource
names and probe outputs report the correct prefixed container/image identifiers.
This is a pre-release user-perspective validation and a natural companion to the
pristine runs.

> **A6 overlap to reconcile:** ₣A6 (rbk-17-mvp-handbook-restart) covers handbook
> territory and may be the better owner of, or already cover, this walk. Decide
> the ownership split when ₣A6 is groomed before slating this here.

## Precursor engineering (lands in ₣BB, not here)

₣BB stays racing for three tail items that gate a clean pristine run but are
ordinary engineering, not release ceremony: a foundry account-state-invalid
retry-tolerance bug fix (401/`ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID` flap), the fast-tier
shellcheck fold + marshal-zero gate, and an RBS0 spec-sync sweep. Confirm these
have landed before racing this heat.

## References

- `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` — the release gate
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.gauntlet.sh` — the gauntlet suite
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ`; marshal-zero signature baked here
- `RELEASE.md` — the runbook under revision
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony;
  pristine pass is a precondition
- ₣BB rbk-15-mvp-release-qualification — built the machinery; holds precursor
  engineering and the git history of the dropped run/doc dockets
- ₣A6 rbk-17-mvp-handbook-restart — handbook territory; reconcile onboarding-walk
  ownership
